preview_text:
The model rates Canada as a strong favorite with an 80% win probability, leaving Bosnia and Herzegovina only a 2% chance of victory. This represents a decisive gap favoring the North American side, though the underdog retains a non-trivial draw probability of 18%.

The spread suggests Canada is expected to control the match decisively. A draw at 18% is plausible but below the baseline; Bosnia and Herzegovina's 2% win chance indicates the model views a home-team upset as highly improbable. The contest is likely to turn on Canada's ability to convert chances rather than surprise competitiveness.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge sharply. Bookmakers price Canada at only 53% to win—27 percentage points lower than the model—while assigning Bosnia and Herzegovina an 20% win probability, roughly nine times the model's estimate. Polymarket prices lie between these extremes but remain closer to the books than to the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence itself warrants attention from those forming their own view.
